title: Ventilator Strategies to Reduce Lung Injury

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Ventilator Strategies to Reduce Lung Injury?
(1) VILI เป็น major driver ของ BPD: Ventilator-induced lung injury — volutrauma, barotrauma, atelectrauma, biotrauma — มีส่วนสำคัญใน BPD pathogenesis มากกว่า O2 toxicity ตามที่เคยเชื่อ. (2) VTV ลด BPD และ death: Volume-targeted ventilation vs pressure-limited: meta-analysis แสดง RR 0.73 (0.59-0.89) สำหรับ BPD or death — ผลดีที่สุดจากการเปลี่ยน mode เดียว. (3) Avoiding intubation = best lung protection: SUPPORT, COIN trials — CPAP ลด BPD rates ในกลุ่ม extremely preterm โดยไม่ต้องเปลี่ยน ventilator mode. (4) HFOV ไม่ได้ดีกว่าเสมอไป: Cochrane 2015 — HFOV vs conventional: no clear BPD benefit เว้นแต่ใช้ร่วมกับ lung recruitment strategy. (5) Surfactant strategy สำคัญเท่า ventilator strategy: INSURE/LISA/MIST timing และ technique ส่งผลต่อ hours of ventilation, BPD, IVH. (6) Permissive hypercapnia: less is more: target 45-55 mmHg ให้ผลดีกว่า aggressive 55-70 mmHg ตาม PHELBI trial — ลด vent settings โดยไม่ต้อง push CO2 สูง.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย mechanisms ของ VILI: volutrauma, barotrauma, atelectrauma, biotrauma, และ oxygen toxicity — แต่ละอย่างส่งผลต่อปอด preterm อย่างไร
เปรียบเทียบ volume-targeted ventilation (VTV) vs pressure-limited ventilation: mechanism, evidence, clinical application ใน NICU
อธิบาย rationale และ clinical application ของ high-frequency oscillatory ventilation (HFOV): lung recruitment strategy, MAP titration, amplitude settings
อธิบาย high-frequency jet ventilation (HFJV): กลไกต่างจาก HFOV อย่างไร, indications, และ evidence ใน neonates
สรุป key evidence สำหรับ early CPAP (SUPPORT/COIN trials) vs surfactant+intubation ใน ELBW — ผลต่อ BPD, IVH, death
เปรียบเทียบ surfactant strategies: prophylactic vs rescue, INSURE, LISA (less-invasive surfactant administration), MIST (minimally invasive surfactant therapy)
อธิบาย concept ของ open lung strategy: lung recruitment + PEEP to prevent atelectrauma — ใน HFOV และ conventional vent
ระบุ target parameters: VT, FiO2, PEEP, MAP ที่เหมาะสมตาม lung disease stage (RDS acute, recovering, BPD)
อธิบาย permissive hypercapnia evidence: PHELBI trial outcomes, appropriate target range, risk threshold ใน preterm
วางแผน weaning strategy จาก mechanical ventilation: criteria for extubation, post-extubation CPAP/HFHC, methylxanthine role
1. Ventilator-Induced Lung Injury (VILI) — Pathophysiology

VILI คือ lung injury ที่เกิดจาก mechanical ventilation เอง — เป็น modifiable contributor ต่อ BPD ที่สำคัญ. เข้าใจ mechanisms ทั้ง 5 ช่วยให้ design ventilator strategy ที่ลด injury ได้ทุก mode ทุก setting.
1.1 Volutrauma — Main Driver of VILI

Definition: overdistension injury จาก excessive tidal volume (VT), ไม่ใช่ excessive pressure. Dreyfuss & Saumon AJRCCM 1992;145:658: classic experiment — rats with high VT (high P + high VT) = severe lung injury; rats with high P แต่ chest strapped (same P, low VT) = minimal injury. สรุป: VT ไม่ใช่ P ที่ทำลาย

Mechanism: overinflated alveoli → stretch → mechanotransduction → inflammatory cytokine release (IL-6, IL-8, TNF-α, MIP-2) → systemic inflammation → multiorgan dysfunction. ↑ vascular permeability → protein-rich edema → surfactant inactivation → ↓ compliance → ↑ VT needed → vicious cycle.

In preterm lung: extreme heterogeneity of compliance — atelectatic (poorly compliant) units adjacent to compliant units. Same inflation pressure → overdistension ของ compliant units ขณะ atelectatic units ยังไม่เปิด. Threshold VT: >5-6 mL/kg in preterm = volutrauma risk
1.2 Barotrauma — Pressure-Mediated Injury
Definition: lung injury from high peak inflation pressure (PIP) — alveolar rupture → air leak syndromes (PIE, pneumothorax, pneumomediastinum, pneumopericardium).

Mechanism: ↑ PIP → alveolar overdistension at peak → alveolar rupture → air tracks along perivascular sheaths → pulmonary interstitial emphysema (PIE): air in lymphatics and connective tissue. PIE → ↓ lung compliance → ↑ vent requirements → worse VILI cycle.

Clinical: PIP >25-28 cmH₂O ใน preterm = ↑ barotrauma risk, แต่ risk depends on lung compliance. Lung จาก surfactant therapy = ↑ compliance → lower PIP threshold for barotrauma. VTV automatically limits VT → indirectly limits peak volume delivered → ↓ barotrauma
1.3 Atelectrauma — Repetitive Collapse-Recruitment Injury

Definition: injury from repeated opening and closing of atelectatic alveoli at each breath — shear forces at air-liquid interface. Mechanism: collapsing alveolus → high surface tension spike → adjacent alveolar wall shear → endothelial + epithelial disruption

This is why adequate PEEP is essential — prevents end-expiratory alveolar collapse → eliminates repetitive shear at each breath. PEEP = atelectrauma prevention; optimal PEEP 5-6 cmH₂O ใน RDS, ↑ to 7-8 cmH₂O ใน BPD with diffuse atelectasis.

Open lung strategy: first recruit (↑ MAP/PEEP to open atelectatic units), then PEEP to keep open. Dalton & Notter Pediatr Crit Care Med 2010;11:S32: lung recruitment + adequate PEEP → ↓ atelectrauma significantly in HFOV.
1.4 Biotrauma — Inflammatory Cascade

Definition: mechanical stretch → activation of mechanosensitive ion channels + stretch-activated receptors → NF-κB activation → ↑ pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-1β, IL-6, IL-8, TNF-α). Cytokines spill into systemic circulation → "biotrauma" = systemic inflammatory response from ventilator

Preterm relevance: preterm lung lacks mature antioxidant defenses → ↑ oxidative stress from VILI + O2 → ↑ biotrauma. Lung lavage IL-8 ↑ in first 24h correlates with BPD development (Baier et al. Pediatrics 1998;101:e16)
Systemic biotrauma → NEC, IVH, ROP — multi-organ targets of lung-originated inflammation. Lung-protective ventilation → ↓ biotrauma → ↓ systemic complications beyond BPD.
1.5 Oxygen Toxicity — Reactive Oxygen Species

Mechanism: FiO2 >0.4-0.5 → ROS generation (superoxide, H₂O₂, hydroxyl radical) → lipid peroxidation + protein oxidation → cell death + ↓ surfactant function. Preterm lung: ↓ superoxide dismutase (SOD), catalase, glutathione peroxidase — immature antioxidant defense.

Target SpO2: 90-95% in preterm <32 wk (avoiding both extremes). BOOST II, COT, SUPPORT: SpO2 85-89% target → ↑ mortality vs 91-95%. Current consensus: 90-95% for ELBW (BAPM 2017, AAP 2016). FiO2 target: wean to <0.3 as soon as possible.

VILI Summary — 5 Mechanisms, 5 Targets
Volutrauma → limit VT ≤4-6 mL/kg (use VTV) | Barotrauma → limit PIP (VTV self-adjusts) | Atelectrauma → adequate PEEP 5-6 cmH₂O, prevent end-expiratory collapse | Biotrauma → minimize vent time; early extubation + CPAP | O2 toxicity → target SpO2 90-95%, wean FiO2 aggressively. แต่ละกลไกเป็น intervention target แยกกัน — อย่า focus เฉพาะ FiO2 หรือ PIP อย่างเดียว; ต้องมอง all 5 simultaneously.
2. Volume-Targeted Ventilation (VTV) — Evidence and Application
2.1 Rationale and Mechanism

VTV = ventilation mode ที่กำหนด tidal volume เป็น target แทน pressure. ใน pressure-limited ventilation (PLV): ถ้า compliance ↑ (surfactant effect, postnatal maturation) → same PIP → ↑ VT delivered → volutrauma. VTV automatically ↓ PIP เมื่อ compliance ↑ → VT delivered คงที่ ≈ target VT regardless of compliance changes.
Mechanism: volume guarantee (VG) in Dräger Babylog, volume control (VC) in SensorMedics: ventilator measures
Expired VT (EVT) each breath → compares to target VT → adjusts PIP for next breath.
If EVT < target: ↑ PIP (up to PIP limit) next breath. If EVT ≥ target: ↓ PIP next breath.
PIP limit set by clinician = safety ceiling. VG uses average ETV of last 10 breaths for feedback loop.
2.2 Evidence — Meta-analysis and Key Trials

Cochrane Review: Wheeler KC, Klingenberg C, et al. 2010 (updated 2017): VTV vs pressure-limited ventilation in preterm. Primary outcome: BPD or death at 36 wk PMA. N=8 RCTs, 768 infants: RR 0.73 (95% CI 0.59-0.89) — NNT ~7. Secondary: ↓ hypocarbia (RR 0.49), ↓ air leak (RR 0.52), ↓ IVH grade III-IV (RR 0.53), ↓ duration of ventilation (mean diff -1.35 days).

Klingenberg C et al. Neonatology 2011;99:352: meta-analysis confirms: VTV → ↓ BPD, ↓ severe IVH, ↓ hypocarbia, ↓ air leaks. Benefit consistent across subgroups (GA, BW, surfactant use, antenatal steroids).

Peng W et al. JAMA Pediatr 2014;168:414: large retrospective cohort, N=2,609 preterm <32 wk: VTV use associated with OR 0.68 (0.50-0.93) for BPD after adjustment. Observational but confirms signal in clinical practice.
McCallion N et al. Cochrane 2005: SIMV + VG vs SIMV alone: ↓ mean airway pressure, ↓ FiO2, faster weaning. VG reduces work of breathing.
2.3 Target VT — Setting in Practice
Target VT ใน preterm RDS: 4-6 mL/kg (weight-based). 4 mL/kg for <28 wk (most vulnerable lung), 5-6 mL/kg for 28-32 wk with RDS.

Why 4-6 mL/kg? Physiologic VT in spontaneously breathing infant = 5-8 mL/kg. Ventilated preterm: (1) ETT leak → some VT lost; (2) lung disease → lower compliance → higher pressures → risk of overdistension. 4-5 mL/kg gives lung protection margin
VT targets by condition:
NEVER use VT >8 mL/kg in preterm — alarm should be set at PIP max such that VT target cannot exceed 8 mL/kg with current compliance.
3. High-Frequency Oscillatory Ventilation (HFOV)
3.1 Mechanism — How HFOV is Different

HFOV delivers very small VT (1-3 mL/kg) at very high frequency (8-15 Hz) — VT < anatomical dead space. Gas exchange occurs via non-convective mechanisms: asymmetric velocity profiles, coaxial flow, pendelluft, cardiogenic mixing, Taylor dispersion, molecular diffusion. CO2 elimination: ∝ frequency × VT² (not VT alone — so ↑ amplitude → ↑ VT → ↑ CO2 elimination).

Mean airway pressure (MAP) in HFOV: controls lung volume (FRC) and oxygenation — analogous to PEEP in conventional. Amplitude (ΔP): controls VT and therefore CO2 elimination. Frequency (Hz): ↑ Hz → ↓ VT per oscillation (inverse) → ↓ CO2 elimination. Lower Hz (8 Hz) = more CO2 out than higher Hz (15 Hz).
3.2 Lung Recruitment Strategy in HFOV

HFOV ไม่ได้ effective โดย default — ต้องใช้ร่วมกับ lung recruitment (open lung approach). Protocol: step-up MAP until FiO2 ↓ → lung opened → step-down MAP slowly (↓ 1-2 cmH₂O/step) → keep FiO2 ≤0.3 → optimal MAP = lowest MAP that maintains FiO2 ≤0.25-0.30 after recruitment

CXR optimal lung expansion: 8-9 posterior ribs on PA view — less = atelectasis (→ ↑ MAP), more = overdistension (→ ↓ MAP). More sensitive: FiO2 as guide — FiO2 ↓ after MAP ↑ = lung opening (not yet overdistended); FiO2 starts ↑ again = overdistension threshold reached.

Amplitude titration: aim for chest wiggle visible to umbilicus in preterm, to groin in term. Blood gas: pCO2 target 45-55 mmHg. Start amplitude at 2× MAP; adjust by 3-5 cmH₂O steps based on pCO2 response.
3.3 Evidence — HFOV vs Conventional Ventilation

Cochrane Review: Cools F et al. 2015 (13 RCTs, N=3,652 infants): HFOV vs conventional ventilation in preterm. No significant difference in BPD (RR 0.95, 95%CI 0.88-1.03). Subgroup with lung recruitment strategy: trend toward ↓ BPD. No ↑ IVH or PVL with HFOV overall.

UK Oscillation Study (UKOS) — Johnson AH, NEJM 2002;347:633: N=797, <29 wk: HFOV vs SIMV. Primary: BPD at 36 wk — no significant difference (RR 1.00). No advantage of HFOV over optimized conventional vent when conventional includes PEEP/VT targets.
HiFO Study — Gerstmann DR, Pediatrics 1996;98:1005: N=176 preterm RDS: HFJV vs conventional. ↓ BPD at 28 days (10% vs 26%, p=0.04). Supports HF modalities in select patients, not universal benefit.

Conclusion: HFOV ไม่ได้ superior ต่อ optimized conventional ventilation ถ้าใช้ VTV + adequate PEEP ในกลุ่มปกติ. HFOV ควรใช้เมื่อ: (1) conventional vent fails (FiO2 high, air leak, severe PIE), (2) severe PPHN with iNO, (3) bilateral PIE, (4) CDH.
4. High-Frequency Jet Ventilation (HFJV)
4.1 Mechanism — Different from HFOV

HFJV (Bunnell Life Pulse) ส่ง active inspiration (jet pulses) via side port of triple lumen ETT adapter ที่ 240-660 breaths/min (4-11 Hz) พร้อมกับ tandem conventional SIMV ช้าๆ (2-4/min สำหรับ sigh breaths). Active inspiration + passive exhalation — ต่างจาก HFOV ที่ active both inspiration AND expiration.

PEEP controlled by separate conventional ventilator running in tandem. Jet pulse duration 0.02 sec fixed, driving pressure (PIP) 10-30 psi = controls CO2 clearance. จุดแข็งของ HFJV: ↑ CO2 clearance ↑ ใน air-leak syndromes (PIE) — small VT delivered bypasses alveolar-bronchial junction where air leak occurs.
4.2 Indications and Evidence

PIE (pulmonary interstitial emphysema): Keszler M et al. J Pediatr 1991;119:85: HFJV vs conventional in severe PIE: PIE resolution 75% vs 35% (p<0.05), ↓ mortality trend. HFJV = preferred mode for severe PIE.
Rescue from conventional failure: infant requiring FiO2 >0.6 + MAP >15 cmH₂O on conventional or HFOV → consider HFJV rescue. Available evidence mostly observational.
CDH (diaphragmatic hernia): HFJV sometimes used as alternative to HFOV. No RCT comparing HFJV vs HFOV vs conventional in CDH specifically.

Practical: HFJV less commonly available than HFOV (only Bunnell Life Pulse device). Requires experienced team. Start PEEP on tandem vent at 3-5 cmH₂O, driving pressure start 15-20 psi, rate 420/min typical. TcCO2 monitoring essential.
5. Early CPAP vs Intubation in Extremely Preterm — Key Trials
5.1 SUPPORT Trial — CPAP vs Surfactant+Intubation
SUPPORT (NICHD Neonatal Research Network) — Finer N et al. NEJM 2010;362:1970: N=1,316 infants 24-27+6 wk. 2×2 factorial: (1) CPAP vs intubation+surfactant at birth; (2) SpO2 target 85-89% vs 91-95%.

Primary outcome: BPD or death at 36 wk PMA — no significant difference: CPAP 47.8% vs surfactant 51.0% (p=0.16). Secondary: CPAP → ↓ need for intubation (48% never intubated in CPAP group), ↓ postnatal steroid use, ↓ days on ventilator.
Interpretation: Early CPAP is non-inferior to intubation+surfactant for BPD or death. CPAP → reduces intubation exposure without compromising outcomes — major practice change.
5.2 COIN Trial — CPAP at 5 cmH₂O vs Intubation
COIN — Morley CJ et al. NEJM 2008;358:700: N=610 infants 25-28+6 wk. CPAP (5 cmH₂O nasal prongs) vs intubation+surfactant at birth.

Primary: BPD or death at 36 wk — no significant difference. Secondary: CPAP → ↑ pneumothorax (9% vs 3%, p=0.001) — attributed to use of only 5 cmH₂O CPAP (↓ adequate). Lesson: CPAP 5 cmH₂O alone may be insufficient; optimal 6-8 cmH₂O especially <26 wk.
5.3 CURPAP Trial — Prophylactic Surfactant before CPAP

CURPAP — Sandri MF et al. Pediatrics 2010;125:e1402: N=208, 25-28+6 wk: prophylactic surfactant → extubate to CPAP (INSURE) vs primary CPAP. No difference in BPD or death. Supports: starting CPAP without mandatory prophylactic surfactant even in 25-28 wk range.

Current Standard: Early CPAP + Selective Surfactant (INSURE or LISA)
Based on SUPPORT + COIN + CURPAP: Start CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O at birth for all <30 wk. Surfactant if: FiO2 >0.3 (within 2h birth), FiO2 >0.25-0.30 (stable on CPAP, per EuroCLEAR protocol). LISA/MIST preferred over INSURE when infant breathing adequately — keep infant on CPAP, avoid intubation, use catheter/NG for surfactant instillation. NNT to prevent one intubation with LISA = 3 (Aldana-Aguirre JC Arch Dis Child FN 2017).
6. Surfactant Delivery Strategies — Timing and Technique
6.1 Prophylactic vs Rescue Surfactant
Prophylactic (<15-30 min of life): Cochrane meta-analysis (Soll RF 2009): prophylactic vs selective rescue — prophylactic ↓ pneumothorax (RR 0.62), ↓ PIE, ↓ mortality. BUT: studies pre-CPAP era.

Post-SUPPORT era reanalysis: prophylactic surfactant no longer advantageous when early CPAP is standard of care. Vermont Oxford Network 2018: hospitals using CPAP-first approach → similar BPD rates regardless of surfactant timing. Current AAP guideline (2014): early CPAP preferred over routine prophylactic surfactant in ≥25 wk
6.2 INSURE — INtubation-SURfactant-Extubation
Protocol: brief intubation → surfactant → rapid extubation to CPAP within 30-60 min. Avoids prolonged ventilation.

Dani C et al. Eur J Pediatr 2004;163:608: INSURE vs continued mechanical ventilation post-surfactant: INSURE → ↓ BPD (RR 0.66), ↓ duration ventilation, ↓ FiO2. Most effective ≥28 wk — failure rate higher in <27 wk (FiO2 >0.4 = INSURE failure predictor).
6.3 LISA / MIST — Less-Invasive Surfactant Administration

LISA (Göttingen method — Kribs A): CPAP maintained, thin catheter (feeding tube 5F) passed through vocal cords under direct laryngoscopy while infant breathing spontaneously → surfactant instilled slowly (1-2 min) → catheter removed → CPAP resumes.
MIST (Dargaville method): Magill forceps guide vascular catheter between vocal cords → surfactant → catheter out. Similar concept to LISA.
AMV Trial — Göpel W et al. Lancet 2011;378:1627: N=220, <27 wk: LISA vs INSURE: LISA → ↓ BPD or death (28% vs 46%, p=0.008). Fewer days on mechanical vent.

Aldana-Aguirre JC et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2017;102:F17 (meta-analysis): 6 RCTs, N=895: LISA vs INSURE: ↓ BPD (RR 0.72), ↓ need for mechanical ventilation, ↓ IVH, ↓ mortality trend. LISA = preferred over INSURE when infant has adequate respiratory drive.
LISA contraindications: absent respiratory effort, FiO2 >0.4 on CPAP (high failure risk), cardiovascular instability. In these → intubate and give surfactant via ETT (INSURE or continue ventilation).
7. Permissive Hypercapnia — Evidence and Limits
7.1 Rationale

Permissive hypercapnia = accepting pCO2 above normal range (>45 mmHg) to allow lower ventilator settings — thereby reducing VILI. Strategy: ↓ RR, ↓ PIP → ↓ VT → ↓ volutrauma/barotrauma, accepting higher pCO2 as tradeoff. แต่ลืมไม่ได้ว่า hypercapnia มีผลเสีย: IVH breakpoint ~52 mmHg (Ch.17 lesson), vasodilation, acidosis.
7.2 PHELBI Trial

PHELBI — Thome UH et al. Lancet Respir Med 2015;3:534: N=359, <28 wk 6d, <72h age. High pCO2 group target: 55-65 mmHg (1-2 wk), then 50-55 (wk 2-4). Mild pCO2 group target: 40-50 mmHg (wk 1), 45-50 (wk 2-4). Primary: ↓ BPD or death at 36 wk PMA — no difference: 67% high vs 63.1% mild (OR 1.18, 95%CI 0.75-1.87)
PHELBI Follow-up — Thome UH et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2017;102:F375: N=271 (2-year outcomes): no difference in NDI (death or neurodevelopmental impairment) — 66.4% high vs 64.7% mild.

Conclusion: High permissive hypercapnia (55-65 mmHg) provides no additional benefit over moderate (40-50 mmHg) — and carries ↑ IVH risk (pCO2 >52 = CBF reactivity breakpoint). Optimal target: 45-55 mmHg (moderate permissive hypercapnia) — allows ↓ vent settings without excess IVH risk.
7.3 Applying Permissive Hypercapnia Safely
Day 1-3 (IVH window): target pCO2 45-52 mmHg MAX — above 52 = IVH breakpoint (Noori 2014). Accept ↑ FiO2 needs rather than push CO2 >52 ใน first 72h.
Day 4 onward: can accept 45-55 mmHg. Avoid <35 (PVL, auto-trigger). Avoid CO2 swings — stability as important as mean value (Fabres 2007: CO2 fluctuation → IVH independently).

Monitoring: transcutaneous CO2 (TcCO2) monitoring preferred — continuous, no sampling pain. Calibrate every 4-6h with blood gas (BGA correlation ±5 mmHg ใน stable infant, ±10 in unstable). TcCO2 lag time ~5 min after ventilator change — avoid rapid over-adjustment.
8. Weaning from Mechanical Ventilation — Strategy
8.1 Criteria for Extubation Readiness
Clinical criteria: FiO2 ≤0.35, MAP ≤8-9 cmH₂O (HFOV) or PIP ≤16-18 cmH₂O (conventional), VT adequate with minimal support, respiratory effort present (spontaneous rate >40/min on minimal backup).
Gas exchange criteria: pCO2 45-55 mmHg without excessive support, pH >7.25, SpO2 90-95% on FiO2 ≤0.35.
When to NOT extubate: hemodynamic instability (dopamine >10 mcg/kg/min), FiO2 >0.40, MAP >8 cmH₂O on HFOV (RDS active), absence of respiratory drive (apnea >8/h on XA), temperature instability.
8.2 Post-Extubation Support

CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O: standard post-extubation support. Avoids re-intubation by maintaining FRC and splinting small airways. NNT = 3 to prevent one extubation failure with CPAP vs no support (Cochrane: Davis PG 2001).

HHHFNC (High-flow nasal cannula 2-8 LPM): non-inferior to CPAP for post-extubation support ≥28 wk (HIPSTER trial — Roberts CT, NEJM 2016;375:1142). Less nasal trauma. Below 28 wk: CPAP preferred — HHHFNC less effective in very preterm. NICU Thailand: Vapotherm or Fisher&Paykel OptiFlow commonly used.

Methylxanthines — caffeine: CAP trial (Schmidt B, NEJM 2006;354:2112): caffeine citrate ↓ BPD (OR 0.63, 0.52-0.76), ↓ PDA treatment, ↓ days on ventilation, ↑ extubation success. Start caffeine 20 mg/kg loading → 5-10 mg/kg/day maintenance, before or at extubation. Continue until 33-34 wk PMA or ≥5 days apnea-free.
8.3 Preventing Extubation Failure
Post-extubation CPAP settings: start at same MAP as pre-extubation (HFOV) or 6-7 cmH₂O (conventional). ↑ CPAP 1 cmH₂O if FiO2 >0.4, ↓ CPAP 1 cmH₂O if infant comfortable + FiO2 <0.21.

Early re-intubation criteria: FiO2 >0.5 for >2h on CPAP 8 cmH₂O, pCO2 >60 with pH <7.20, apnea >6 requiring stimulation/PPV, hemodynamic instability. Delaying re-intubation when criteria met = increased VILI + BPD: lung already injured before rescue vent initiated.
9. NICU Practical — Lung-Protective Protocol at KCMH NICU

Day 0 (delivery room): CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O via neopuff T-piece or RAM cannula for all <30 wk. SpO2 blender: start FiO2 0.21 (term)/0.30 (preterm), titrate to NRP 2020 SpO2 targets (1 min: 60-65%, 5 min: 80-85%, 10 min: 85-95%).

NICU first 2h: if FiO2 >0.30 on CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O → surfactant. Prefer LISA/MIST if spontaneous breathing adequate. If LISA not available → INSURE: intubate → surfactant (Survanta 100 mg/kg or Curosurf 200 mg/kg) → extubate to CPAP within 30-60 min.

If intubated: start VG (volume guarantee) immediately. Set: VT target 4-5 mL/kg (ELBW), 5-6 mL/kg (VLBW). PIP limit 22-25 cmH₂O. PEEP 5-6 cmH₂O. Rate 30-40/min (A/C mode). FiO2 titrate to SpO2 90-95%. TcCO2 monitor if available.
Permissive hypercapnia: target pCO2 45-52 mmHg (Day 1-3), 45-55 mmHg (Day 4+). Avoid >52 ใน 3 วันแรก (IVH risk). Avoid <35 (PVL + auto-trigger). Check BGA 4-6 hourly ใน acute phase.

HFOV indication: FiO2 >0.6 + MAP >15 cmH₂O on conventional + no PIE → lung recruitment HFOV. Or bilateral PIE on CXR → HFOV or HFJV. Start MAP = conventional MAP + 2 cmH₂O. Amplitude 2× MAP. Frequency 10 Hz preterm, 8 Hz term. Recruit by ↑ MAP 1-2 cmH₂O q15-30 min until FiO2 ↓.
Caffeine: load 20 mg/kg IV over 30 min → maintenance 5-10 mg/kg/day — start by 24h of life (before extubation attempt). Continue to 34-35 wk PMA if no apnea.
Extubation readiness check daily from Day 3: PIP ≤16, VT adequate, FiO2 ≤0.30, respiratory effort adequate, caffeine on board → extubate to CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O → watch 4h for FiO2 ↑ or CO2 ↑.
Post-extubation HHHFNC: if ≥28 wk + CPAP weaned to 4 cmH₂O → step to HHHFNC 3-6 LPM → wean flow 1 LPM/day. <28 wk: stay on CPAP until stable ≥2 wk post-extubation before attempting HHHFNC.
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับใช้พรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — VTV คือ Default Mode สำหรับ Ventilated Preterm: ทำไม และ Target คืออะไร
Cochrane 2017 (Wheeler): VTV vs PLV → NNT ~7 เพื่อป้องกัน 1 case ของ BPD or death. กลไก: compliance ↑ (surfactant response, recovery) → PLV ส่ง VT เพิ่มขึ้นโดยอัตโนมัติ → volutrauma; VTV ↓ PIP เมื่อ compliance ↑ → VT คงที่. Target: 4-5 mL/kg ใน ELBW, 5-6 mL/kg ใน VLBW. PIP limit 22-25 cmH₂O. หลังสร้าง VG mode: monitor delivered VT on ventilator display — ถ้า VT consistently >6 mL/kg → ↓ target; ถ้า PIP hitting ceiling → reassess for PIE/mucus/ETT obstruction.

PEARL 2 — LISA/MIST = New Gold Standard: ใครได้ ใครไม่ได้
Aldana-Aguirre meta-analysis 2017: LISA vs INSURE → ↓ BPD (RR 0.72), ↓ vent days, NNT=3 to prevent 1 intubation. ทำได้เมื่อ: FiO2 0.30-0.40 on CPAP, spontaneous breathing adequate, hemodynamically stable. อย่าทำเมื่อ: FiO2 >0.45, absent respiratory effort, cardiovascular instability, no person experienced in laryngoscopy without intubation. Technique: pre-medicate? — LISA ดั้งเดิมไม่ใช้ sedation (Göttingen); แต่ pain scale evidence → consider sucrose + gentle handling. Catheter depth = ETT formula (BW+6 cm from lip). Instill slowly 1-2 mL/min.

PEARL 3 — HFOV ≠ Magic: ต้องทำ Lung Recruitment Strategy
Cochrane 2015 (Cools): HFOV vs conventional → no BPD benefit UNLESS lung recruitment used. ขั้นตอน recruitment: HFOV → start MAP = conventional MAP + 2 cmH₂O → ↑ MAP 1-2 cmH₂O q15-30 min → watch FiO2 ↓ (lung opening sign) → stop ↑ MAP เมื่อ FiO2 ≤0.25 → CXR 8-9 posterior ribs. ถ้าไม่ทำ recruitment: HFOV ไม่ดีกว่า conventional และอาจแย่กว่า (lung heterogeneous, CO2 clearance poor). หลัง recruitment: MAP ↓ 1 cmH₂O/step q4-6h เมื่อ FiO2 stable — avoid premature PEEP step-down.

PEARL 4 — Early Caffeine + Extubation = Most Powerful BPD Prevention Combo
CAP trial (Schmidt 2006): caffeine → ↓ BPD OR 0.63 (0.52-0.76), ↓ PDA tx need, ↓ vent days. เริ่ม caffeine ตั้งแต่ Day 1 ก่อนพยายาม extubate (ไม่ใช่ start วันที่ extubate). Loading 20 mg/kg IV → maintenance 5-10 mg/kg/day. Protocol extubation daily: PIP ≤16, VT adequate, FiO2 ≤0.30 → extubate → CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O → watch 4h. อย่า delay extubation ถ้า criteria met: ทุกวันบน ventilator = VILI exposure + biotrauma. Re-intubate ถ้า fail — ไม่ใช่ shame, เป็น appropriate management.

PEARL 5 — VT >6 mL/kg + pCO2 <35 mmHg = Auto-Trigger + Volutrauma: Check ETT Leak
ทั้ง 2 problems (เกินทั้ง VT และ CO2 ต่ำ) อาจเกิดจาก auto-triggering — ventilator triggered by artifact (cardiac oscillation, ETT leak) → inappropriate extra breath → ↑ effective VT → hyperventilation → ↓ pCO2 + iatrogenic volutrauma. Check: ETT leak? (auscultate, seal test) → ↑ cuff pressure if cuffed ETT; ETT size change if uncuffed. Trigger sensitivity too high? → ↓ trigger sensitivity (↑ threshold). Always correlate delivered VT on display (should be 4-6 mL/kg) with clinical status + blood gas simultaneously. Auto-trigger = fixable cause of unexpected pCO2 <35.
11. Evidence Update — Key Citations
Bancalari E & Claure N. The Newborn Lung Ch.18, 2019: Primary reference — comprehensive framework for lung-protective ventilation strategies in preterm neonates.

Wheeler KC, Klingenberg C, et al. Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2010: VTV vs pressure-limited ventilation: RR 0.73 for BPD or death. Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs, N=768. Foundational evidence for VTV as standard of care.
Dreyfuss D, Saumon G. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 1998;157:294: Classic VILI mechanism — volutrauma vs barotrauma: VT (not pressure) is the primary determinant of lung injury. Chest-strapped rat model.

Finer N et al. (SUPPORT). NEJM 2010;362:1970: N=1,316, 24-27+6 wk: early CPAP non-inferior to intubation+surfactant for BPD or death. 48% CPAP group never intubated. Changed practice to CPAP-first approach.
Morley CJ et al. (COIN). NEJM 2008;358:700: N=610, 25-28+6 wk: CPAP vs intubation: no BPD difference; CPAP 9% pneumothorax vs 3% — attributed to 5 cmH₂O CPAP being inadequate.

Aldana-Aguirre JC et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2017;102:F17: LISA vs INSURE meta-analysis: N=895, 6 RCTs: ↓ BPD (RR 0.72), ↓ vent days, NNT=3 to prevent one intubation. Establishes LISA as preferred surfactant strategy.
Göpel W et al. (AMV Trial). Lancet 2011;378:1627: N=220, <27 wk: LISA vs INSURE: ↓ BPD or death 28% vs 46% (p=0.008). First major RCT validating LISA approach in ELBW.
Thome UH et al. (PHELBI). Lancet Respir Med 2015;3:534: N=359, <28+6 wk: high vs mild permissive hypercapnia: no BPD benefit. High CO2 target not beneficial — moderate target (45-55 mmHg) sufficient.
Schmidt B et al. (CAP Trial). NEJM 2006;354:2112: N=2,006 VLBW: caffeine vs placebo: ↓ BPD (OR 0.63), ↓ vent days, ↑ extubation success. Caffeine = most evidence-based single intervention to ↓ BPD.
Roberts CT et al. (HIPSTER). NEJM 2016;375:1142: N=564, ≥28 wk: HHHFNC vs CPAP post-extubation: non-inferior. HHHFNC acceptable alternative in ≥28 wk — not validated for ELBW <28 wk.
Cools F et al. Cochrane 2015 (HFOV vs CMV): 13 RCTs, N=3,652: no significant BPD benefit from HFOV overall; trend toward benefit with lung recruitment strategy. HFOV not superior to optimized CMV.
Keszler M et al. J Pediatr 1991;119:85: HFJV vs conventional in severe PIE: PIE resolution 75% vs 35%. Establishes HFJV as preferred mode for bilateral PIE.
Peng W et al. JAMA Pediatr 2014;168:414: N=2,609, <32 wk: VTV association with ↓ BPD (OR 0.68, 0.50-0.93). Large observational study confirming Cochrane RCT findings in clinical practice.

Sandri MF et al. (CURPAP). Pediatrics 2010;125:e1402: N=208, 25-28+6 wk: prophylactic surfactant+INSURE vs early CPAP: no BPD difference. Supports CPAP-first approach without mandatory prophylactic surfactant.
Noori S et al. Acta Paediatr 2014;103:e334: CO2 breakpoint ~52 mmHg for IVH risk in preterm — referenced in permissive hypercapnia ceiling decisions (from NL Day 17).
Fabres J et al. Pediatrics 2007;119:299: CO2 fluctuation magnitude independently predicts IVH — importance of CO2 stability, not just mean target.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: อธิบาย mechanism ที่ VTV ลด BPD ได้มากกว่า PLV (pressure-limited ventilation) — ทำไม PLV เป็น problem เมื่อ lung compliance ดีขึ้น?

ตอบ: PLV problem: กำหนด PIP คงที่ → เมื่อ compliance ↑ (surfactant response, day 2-3 recovery): same PIP → ↑ delivered VT → volutrauma. Clinician มักไม่ทราบ compliance เปลี่ยน until pCO2 ↓↓ หรือ CXR overdistended. VTV ป้องกัน: measures expired VT → ↓ PIP automatically when compliance ↑ → VT target maintained ≤5-6 mL/kg. Cochrane 2017: RR 0.73 BPD or death, ↓ hypocarbia (RR 0.49), ↓ air leak (RR 0.52), ↓ IVH grade III-IV (RR 0.53). Benefits mediated by preventing inadvertent hyperventilation + volutrauma.
Q: อธิบาย LISA technique และ ระบุข้อห้าม (contraindications) ที่ทำให้ต้องเปลี่ยนไป intubate แทน

ตอบ: LISA technique: infant on CPAP (breathing spontaneously) → direct laryngoscopy → pass 5F feeding catheter between cords → surfactant instilled slowly (1-2 mL/min, ~2 min) → catheter removed → CPAP resumes. No intubation, no PPV interruption. Pre-medication: optional (sucrose + non-nutritive sucking; avoid sedation ที่ suppress respiratory drive). Contraindications → intubate instead: FiO2 >0.45 on CPAP 7 cmH₂O (high failure risk), absent spontaneous respiratory effort, cardiovascular instability, no operator experienced in laryngoscopy without intubation. Aldana-Aguirre 2017: NNT=3 vs INSURE; AMV 2011: ↓ BPD 28% vs 46%.
Q: ทารก 26 wk, ชั่วโมงที่ 4 ของชีวิต, intubated on VTV 5 mL/kg, PEEP 5, FiO2 0.45. Monitor แสดง delivered VT 8.5 mL/kg, pCO2 32 mmHg. อธิบาย diagnosis และ management

ตอบ: Diagnosis: auto-trigger + ETT leak — delivered VT 8.5 mL/kg (>6 = volutrauma territory) + pCO2 32 (hypocapnia = over-ventilation). Mechanism: ETT leak → gas escaping → flow sensor misreads → ventilator delivers extra breath (auto-trigger) → effective rate ↑ → VT per target delivered + leak-induced volume = 8.5 mL/kg total → hyperventilation. Management: (1) Check ETT position + assess audible leak (should be <10%). (2) ↓ trigger sensitivity. (3) ↑ ETT size by 0.5 mm if uncuffed + significant leak. (4) Re-check pCO2 in 30 min. Target: delivered VT 4-5 mL/kg, pCO2 45-52 mmHg (Day 1).
Q: อธิบาย lung recruitment strategy ใน HFOV — ทำไม HFOV โดยไม่ทำ lung recruitment ถึงไม่ได้ผล?

ตอบ: Cochrane 2015 (Cools): HFOV vs CMV — overall no BPD benefit; trend benefit only ใน studies ที่ใช้ lung recruitment. ทำไม: HFOV ส่ง VT เล็ก (1-3 mL/kg) via oscillation → ถ้า lung ยังมี atelectasis (underrecruited) → oscillation bounces off collapsed region → CO2 clearance poor + oxygenation poor → clinician ↑ FiO2 + amplitude (worsening). Recruitment protocol: start HFOV MAP = last conventional MAP + 2 → ↑ MAP 1-2 cmH₂O q15-30 min → watch FiO2 ↓ sign = lung opening → stop when FiO2 ≤0.25 or CXR 8-9 posterior ribs → then step-down MAP 1 cmH₂O q4-6h to find lowest MAP maintaining FiO2 ≤0.30. This open-close-open cycling = essence of HFOV benefit.
Q: SUPPORT trial (Finer 2010) — primary endpoint ไม่ significant แต่ทำไมยังแนะนำ CPAP-first ในปัจจุบัน?

ตอบ: Primary outcome (BPD or death): CPAP 47.8% vs surfactant 51.0% — p=0.16 (NS). แต่ secondary outcomes สำคัญ: 48% ของ CPAP group ไม่เคยต้องถูก intubate, ↓ postnatal steroid use, ↓ days on vent, ↓ death in CPAP group (numerically). Non-inferiority interpretation: CPAP ไม่แย่กว่า intubation+surfactant — แต่ลด intubation exposure significantly. Principle of least harm: ทุก intubation = VILI risk, infection risk, sedation exposure. Combined with COIN + CURPAP + LISA evidence: CPAP-first + selective surfactant (LISA/INSURE) = current standard for <30 wk.
Q: CAP trial — caffeine mechanism ใน reducing BPD? และ optimal timing ของ initiation?

ตอบ: Mechanism: caffeine → adenosine receptor antagonist → (1) ↑ respiratory drive → ↓ apnea → ↑ extubation success. (2) ↑ diaphragm contractility. (3) Anti-inflammatory (↓ IL-1β, TNF-α via phosphodiesterase inhibition → ↓ biotrauma). (4) Bronchodilator (mild). Schmidt 2006: OR 0.63 (0.52-0.76) for BPD — largest single intervention effect vs any other strategy in preterm RCT. Optimal timing: start by Day 1-3 of life, ไม่ใช่รอ Day 7 ก่อน extubate. CAP trial: benefit seen regardless of when started, แต่ earlier = more vent-free days. Load 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5-10 mg/kg/day. Continue to 33-35 wk PMA.
Q: ระบุ VILI mechanisms ทั้ง 5 และ ventilator strategy ที่แก้แต่ละ mechanism

ตอบ: 1. Volutrauma → VTV (limit VT 4-6 mL/kg). 2. Barotrauma → VTV (↓ PIP when compliance ↑) + PIP limit setting. 3. Atelectrauma → adequate PEEP 5-6 cmH₂O + open lung strategy (recruitment). 4. Biotrauma → minimize vent time: early extubation + CPAP + caffeine + LISA (avoid intubation). 5. Oxygen toxicity → target SpO2 90-95%, wean FiO2 aggressively, avoid FiO2 >0.4 prolonged. Each mechanism has a specific intervention — lung-protective ventilation means addressing all 5 simultaneously, not just FiO2 or PIP alone.
Q: HFJV vs HFOV — ระบุ 2 indications ที่ HFJV ได้เปรียบและ mechanism ที่ทำให้ได้เปรียบ

ตอบ: Indication 1 — bilateral PIE: Keszler 1991: HFJV → 75% PIE resolution vs 35% conventional. Mechanism: HFJV small VT (1-2 mL/kg) delivered via side-port jet bypasses large airway–alveolar junction where PIE develops → less gas driven into interstitial emphysematous tracks → PIE decompresses. Indication 2 — rescue from conventional failure when HFOV not available or failed: HFJV different mechanism (active inspiration + passive expiration vs HFOV active both) → may improve CO2 clearance in specific pathologies (e.g., air-trapping where active expiration worsens gas trapping). Practical: HFJV requires Bunnell Life Pulse + tandem conventional ventilator + experienced team.
Q: อธิบาย oxygen targeting evidence (BOOST II, COT, SUPPORT SpO2 arm) — ทำไม target 85-89% ถึงมีปัญหา?

ตอบ: Three trials (BOOST II UK, BOOST II Australia, COT): combined N>4,000, <28 wk. SpO2 target 85-89% vs 91-95%. 85-89% group: ↑ mortality (RR ~1.25, p=0.02), ↑ NEC. 91-95% group: ↑ treated ROP (not significant increase in severe ROP after reanalysis with corrected oximetry). BOOST III (UK ongoing): reconfirming 91-95% as safe target. Current consensus: SpO2 90-95% for preterm <32 wk on supplemental O2 (AAP 2016, BAPM 2017). Avoid <88% (hypoxia → mortality + NEC) and avoid >98% (↑ ROP, O2 toxicity). In PPHN: maintain 95-99% until iNO weaned.
NL Day 19 Preview

The Newborn Lung Ch.19 — Respiratory Care in the Delivery Room
พรุ่งนี้: Respiratory management in the delivery room — the first minutes of life — เชื่อมกับบทนี้ (LISA/MIST เริ่มตั้งแต่ delivery room เมื่อทำ INSURE), Ch.17 (PBCC + cord clamping timing), และ NRP 2020. หัวข้อหลัก: initial stabilization tools (T-piece resuscitator, PPV technique, PEEP at birth), oxygen targeting SpO2 reference ranges, sustained inflation (SI) evidence — 2019 SAIL trial safety concerns, laryngoscopy + intubation DR technique, delivery room surfactant (INSURE vs LISA in delivery room), resuscitation at threshold of viability (<25 wk), mask leak and VT delivery issues, delivery room CPAP pressure settings. เชื่อมกับวันนี้: LISA ที่ทำใน delivery room หรือในชั่วโมงแรก = earliest lung-protective intervention; CPAP 6-7 cmH₂O เริ่มตั้งแต่ delivery room = atelectrauma prevention ตั้งแต่ first breath.
